title: CLI accuracy evaluations process samples sequentially instead of concurrently
description:
## Problem
Running `plexus` CLI accuracy evaluations appears to process items serially. Throughput is much lower than expected and logs show one item entering score processing every ~2 seconds instead of concurrent scheduling.

## Expected
Samples should be evaluated in parallel up to configured concurrency limits.

## Reproduction
Run CLI evaluation with ~20 labeled samples and inspect timestamps around `Resolved Item` and `Starting workflow execution` log lines.

## Definition of Done
- Identify where sequential behavior is introduced in evaluation pipeline
- Update implementation so item-level work is scheduled concurrently
- Verify with a local run or targeted test/log evidence
- Document behavior and any concurrency limits

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Started investigation. Initial symptom: item-level scoring appears to be serialized in CLI accuracy evaluation; tracing code path in plexus/cli/evaluation/evaluations.py and evaluation runtime to confirm where work scheduling happens.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Root cause identified in AccuracyEvaluation.score_text: synchronous Item.get_by_id and Item.find_by_identifier calls happened before the first await, which blocks the event loop and serializes task startup. Patched both calls to run via await asyncio.to_thread(...) so lookup latency does not block other evaluation tasks. Validation: python -m py_compile plexus/Evaluation.py passes.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Landed on branch fix/evaluations-speed in commit 948f4080. Change: moved blocking item fetches (Item.get_by_id and Item.find_by_identifier) onto asyncio.to_thread inside AccuracyEvaluation.score_text to restore true concurrent scheduling across samples. User confirmed significant runtime improvement (>2x faster).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Closure note: completed and landed in commit 948f4080 on fix/evaluations-speed; module compile check passed; user confirmed major runtime improvement.
